Based on my visual inspection of the molecule, here are the results:

## Neighbour Atoms of Atom Index 5 (N)

Atom index 5 is indeed **N** (Nitrogen), shown as a blue atom on the right side of the molecule.

The neighbour atoms covalently connected to N:5 are:

| Atomic Index | Element |
|--------------|---------|
| 6 | C (Carbon) |
| 7 | H (Hydrogen) |
| 33 | H (Hydrogen) |
| 34 | H (Hydrogen) |

## Summary
The nitrogen atom (N:5) has **4 neighbour atoms**: one carbon atom (C:6) and three hydrogen atoms (H:7, H:33, H:34). This forms a typical ammonium-like structure where the nitrogen is bonded to one carbon and three hydrogens.
